history.content:
<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The safe open cell to my right is the best immediate move to continue toward the visible goal while avoiding the lava below.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Turning left would face me toward the open corridor on the left, which is safer than the lava-adjacent path and may help me reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward and the cell directly ahead is a wall, so forward is blocked. The safest immediate way to keep progressing is to turn right, which points me toward the open space instead of the blocked wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. Turning left will orient me toward the open corridor on the left side, away from the blocked path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up, and the cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Turning right will face me toward open floor cells, which is the best safe progress from here.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up and the cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot advance. Turning left is the safer way to face the open path instead of the blocked corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. Turning right will orient me toward the open floor area and is the best next step toward navigating around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up and the cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Turning left will face me toward the open path on the left side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front is a wall. I need to reorient first, and turning right should face me toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up, and the cell directly ahead is a wall. The open and safe space is to my left, so I should turn left to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is still visible ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front is open floor. Moving forward is safe and brings me closer to the goal corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead, but the cells directly in front/right path contain lava, so I should avoid moving forward. Turning left should orient me toward the safer open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am in a corridor with walls blocking most directions. The cell ahead is open floor, so moving forward is safe and progresses through the passage.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The local view is unchanged, which suggests I likely did not move or I am still facing the same corridor. The cell directly ahead is open floor, so the safest progress is to move forward again.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The view remains the same and the corridor ahead is still open. I will continue forward to advance through the passage.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
